content: Most existing explorations of LLM self-reflection mechanisms treat self-reflection as a single monolithic capability, while Self-Refine demonstrates that effective self-improvement can be achieved through a lightweight, iterative multi-step feedback loop without extra training. Combining these two ideas, we can hypothesize that many current underperforming LLM self-reflection mechanisms fail not because the model lacks inherent reflective ability, but because they attempt to implement reflection as a one-step global check rather than breaking it into the iterative, incremental self-feedback structure that Self-Refine proves effective.
